key_departures.impact: Traded at the 2025 trade deadline in a full teardown. Gardner was a 2x All-Pro and franchise cornerstone. The Jets received two first-round picks in return. His departure left a massive hole at CB1 entering the offseason.

Traded at the 2025 trade deadline. Williams was the best interior defensive lineman on the roster and an All-Pro-caliber pass rusher. The Jets received a 2026 second-round pick, 2027 first-round pick, and DT Mazi Smith. Departure gutted IDL depth and pass-rush production.

Traded in a player-for-player swap for DT T'Vondre Sweat. Johnson had just 3 sacks in 2025 post-Achilles tear and was entering his expensive $13.4M fifth-year option. Departure further depleted an already thin edge room, escalating EDGE as a critical need and signaling the Jets will target EDGE at No. 2 overall.

Starting left guard for two seasons, never missed a game. Signed a 3yr/$30M deal with Baltimore — a significant overpay relative to his market value (~$6.5M/yr projected). Departure creates a starting guard vacancy on the left side.

Former first-round pick signed a 3yr/$42M deal (up to $48M) with the Patriots. Missed all of 2025 with a triceps tear — third major injury in five NFL seasons. Combined with Simpson's departure, the Jets lost both starting guards in one day. Jets reportedly never prioritized re-signing him.

Signed a 2yr/$17M deal with the Browns. Former All-Pro LB in 2023, but regressed significantly in 2025 and was briefly benched. His departure was expected and planned for. Demario Davis arrival directly replaces him.
NFL's most accurate kicker (96.3%) since 2023. Signed with the Falcons. His departure creates a significant need at kicker. The Jets could not retain him despite interest.
Depth safety entered free agency. Jets let him walk without a tender. Moderate loss of depth.

signings.trade_details: Acquired via trade from Miami Dolphins in exchange for a 2026 seventh-round pick (originally from Chargers). Fitzpatrick signed a new 3yr/$40M extension upon arrival.
signings.analysis: A steal. The Jets traded a 7th-round pick for a 5x Pro Bowler and 3x All-Pro who graded 81.8 on PFF in 2025. The 3-year extension with ~$20M guaranteed locks in a true starting safety. Fitzpatrick is the clear S1 and immediately becomes a top-3 player on the roster. S need goes from critical to fully addressed — Jets may still want developmental depth but the starter spot is solved.

signings.trade_details: Acquired via trade from Las Vegas Raiders. Jets sent a 2026 6th-round pick and received Smith plus a 2027 7th-round pick. Raiders paying the majority of Smith's prior extension; Jets' cost is just over veteran minimum (~$1.3M).
signings.analysis: At near-minimum cost, Smith provides an experienced starter for 2026. However, a 35-year-old on a 1-year near-minimum deal is transparently a bridge. Smith had a disastrous 2025 campaign with Las Vegas. The Jets are widely expected to target a franchise QB via trade or draft. QB remains a genuine long-term need — this move partially addresses the 2026 season only. Short-term solution, not long-term answer.

signings.trade_details: Acquired in a player-for-player trade sending Jermaine Johnson II to the Titans. Sweat has 2 years remaining on his rookie contract (cost-controlled). Exact rookie-scale figures: 2026 and 2027 year on original 2024 2nd-round deal — estimated ~$3.5M per year.
signings.analysis: Excellent value acquisition. Sweat graded 5th among all DTs on PFF in 2025 (83.4 grade) and 4th in run defense. He is 24 years old with two more cheap contract years remaining — perfect for a rebuild. However, he is not a full-time player (played ~50% of snaps in 2025) and is not a pass-rush threat of Quinnen Williams' caliber. Adds with Onyemata (signed FA) helps meaningfully. IDL partially addressed with strong upside; no elite pass-rusher interior like Williams, but run defense is solidified.

The Jets' top EDGE signing. Ossai, 25, posted 5 sacks in each of the last two seasons and had career highs in pressure rate and pass-rush win rate in 2025. The 3-year deal with $22.5M guaranteed signals a real investment, not a bridge. He is projected as the starting defensive end in the Jets' new 3-4 front. However, 5 sacks is modest production and the Jets' EDGE room was so depleted (second-fewest sacks in NFL at 26 in 2025) that EDGE remains a critical need that still demands the No. 2 overall pick. Partially addresses the starter problem — a good player, not a difference-maker yet.

Onyemata is a large run-stuffing DT (6-4, 310 lbs) who pairs with T'Vondre Sweat to address the Jets' 29th-ranked run defense. His 1-year deal at $10.5M with $9.65M guaranteed is a significant commitment for a 33-year-old — signals a clear starter role. However, at 33 on a 1-year deal, this is a bridge with no long-term certainty. IDL depth is improved; long-term IDL need remains for the draft.

Davis returns for a third stint with the Jets in a reunion with head coach Aaron Glenn (who coached him 3 years in New Orleans). The 2-year/$22M deal with $15M guaranteed is a significant investment. Davis graded 81.4 on PFF in 2025 and was elite in run defense. However, at 37 (year 15 of his career), pass-coverage metrics declined and his longevity is uncertain beyond 2026. This is a strong starter-level signing but the age and 2-year structure signal a bridge that buys time, not a permanent solution. LB is improved but the team should still target a long-term LB in the draft.

Enagbare is a durable rotational edge rusher who has never missed a game in 4 NFL seasons (68 of 68 possible games). He recorded 11.5 career sacks in 68 games, projecting as a rotational piece in the Jets' 3-4 front, not a starter. His 1-year prove-it structure signals depth/upside play, not a permanent solution. Raises the floor of the EDGE room but EDGE at No. 2 overall remains essentially locked in. EDGE need essentially unchanged at the top tier.

Parham replaces the departed John Simpson at left guard. He started 63 of 64 career games with the Raiders, graded PFF's 14th-best LG in 2025, and is ascending at just 26. The 2-year/$16M base deal (all $7.5M guaranteed in Year 1 only — no guaranteed money in Year 2) is solid value but the lack of Year 2 guarantees reflects modest commitment. Parham is slated to start at LG immediately. Partially addresses the double-guard loss — replaces Simpson reasonably well, but the IOL need created by Vera-Tucker's departure remains. The Jets still need another interior lineman (guard or center depth) via draft or additional FA.

Signed to pair with Fitzpatrick at safety. Belton's 1-year deal worth up to $6M is a depth/competition signing for the S2 job. He is a strong tackler (95% efficiency per NGS, 4th among all safeties in 2025) and a core special teamer. One-year structure means he is not the long-term answer at S2 but raises the floor significantly. Good value signing.

Wright led all CBs with 5 interceptions in 2025 (tied for the NFL lead). The Jets — who failed to record a single interception all season (an NFL first) — are adding a proven ballhawk at extremely modest cost: 1 year, $3.5M fully guaranteed. This is a tremendous value signing. However, a 1-year deal at depth money signals competition and rotational role, not a CB1 replacement for Sauce Gardner. CB remains a critical need — this is ball-hawk depth but the starting CB spot is still wide open.

Re-signed on a surprise 1-year deal worth up to $5.25M. Cisco is depth/competition behind Fitzpatrick and Belton. 1-year deal with minimal guarantees ($1.25M) confirms this is a depth signing only. Minimal impact on the need picture.

Re-signed as the Jets' 6th offensive lineman and swing tackle. Mitchell has 17 career starts, provides experienced OL depth and position flexibility. 1-year deal at modest cost — pure depth retention. OT was not a pressing need (starters Olu Fashanu and Armand Membou are locked in) and this doesn't change the need picture.

Re-signed swing tackle with 92 career games and 60 NFL starts, though he didn't play a single offensive snap in 2025 due to the health of the starters. Pure veteran OT depth retention. No impact on need picture.

Re-signed after a strong late-season showing (PFF's 8th-highest graded LB in Weeks 14-18). Plays 81% of special teams snaps and provides LB depth behind Demario Davis. 1-year depth deal — does not change the LB picture materially but is solid depth/ST retention.

Re-signed elite kick returner who is arguably the best in the NFL when healthy. The Jets retained both of their dynamic returners (Nwangwu + Isaiah Williams) for 2026. This is a special teams depth signing, not a RB need move.

Signed to attempt to replace the departed Nick Folk. York is a 2022 Browns 4th-round pick with a career 73% FG accuracy — well below the ~85% NFL average. He spent 2025 on the Saints' practice squad without a regular-season snap. This is a competition/camp signing, not a solution. A meaningful step down from Folk (96.3% accuracy since 2023). K remains an unresolved need — the Jets will likely continue searching for upgrades.

Re-signed fullback on a fully guaranteed $1.5M deal. Beck has a role in new OC Frank Reich's offense (Reich ran fullback-heavy sets in Indianapolis). Minimum-tier retention, no impact on positional needs.

Re-signed depth TE who appeared in only 4 games in 2025 after a waiver-wire addition. Frank Reich connection (Woods was his 3rd-round pick in Indianapolis in 2021) likely drove the re-signing. Minimum-tier depth retention.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Minkah Fitzpatrick trade (3yr/$40M, 5x Pro Bowler, 81.8 PFF grade in 2025) acquired for a 7th-round pick is a slam dunk. S1 is elite. Dane Belton and Andre Cisco provide legitimate S2 competition. Safety is no longer a draft need of any urgency.

Demario Davis (2yr/$22M, $15M gtd) is a legitimate starting LB even at 37 — 81.4 PFF grade in 2025, elite run defender. Mykal Walker retained for depth/ST. However, Davis is 37 on a 2-year bridge and Quincy Williams' departure creates a medium-term hole. The Jets should still draft a LB in rounds 3-5 for development, but it is no longer a premium early-round need.

T'Vondre Sweat (via trade, 2 cheap contract years remaining, 5th-best DT on PFF in 2025) + David Onyemata (1yr/$10.5M, clear run-stuffer) meaningfully addresses the void left by Quinnen Williams. However, neither is a Williams-level pass rusher and Onyemata is 33 on a 1-year bridge. IDL should still be targeted in the middle rounds (Rounds 2-4). Not a crisis, but long-term starter quality remains a need.

Despite signing Ossai (3yr/$34.5M) and Enagbare (1yr/$10M), EDGE remains the No. 1 draft priority. The Jets recorded the second-fewest sacks in the NFL (26) in 2025. Trading Johnson confirmed the No. 2 overall pick is likely an EDGE. Ossai is a real starter but modest producer (5 sacks); Enagbare is rotational. The unit desperately needs an elite, disruptive EDGE anchor. This is the clearest draft-day need on the board.

Sauce Gardner was traded at the deadline and is now a Colt. Nahshon Wright (1yr/$3.5M fully gtd) is a legitimate ball-hawk but on a depth-value deal with no guarantee of the starting role. The Jets failed to record a single interception all of 2025. CB1 is entirely unsolved. No multi-year investment was made at this position. CB should be a top 2-round target and remains Tier 1.

Both starting guards (Simpson to Baltimore, Vera-Tucker to New England) departed. Dylan Parham (2yr/$16M, starting LG) partially fills the Simpson void but Year 2 has no guarantees. No center depth addition of note beyond re-signing Max Mitchell as a 6th lineman. The Jets' young core of Fashanu/Membou/Tippmann is solid at OT and RG, but LG and interior depth remain genuine needs for the draft (Rounds 2-4).

Geno Smith (1yr/~$1.3M, Raiders paying most of contract) is a stop-gap bridge. At 35 on a near-minimum deal, this is explicitly a 1-year solution. The Jets have the No. 2 overall pick and significant future draft capital. QB remains a legitimate long-term need but the 2026 season is stabilized at near-zero cost. If EDGE is taken at No. 2, QB becomes the clear priority for remainder of draft or via trade.

The Jets made no WR additions in free agency. Their receiver room (Garrett Wilson + depth) remains a genuine concern, and beat reporters flagged WR as the one glaring hole Mougey didn't address. WR need is unchanged — should target in Rounds 2-4 of the draft or via trade.

Nick Folk (3x most accurate kicker since 2023) departed for Atlanta. Cade York signed on a minimum deal is a camp competition with a 73% career FG rate — well below average. The Jets may still add a legitimate kicker. K is an unresolved, modest need.

key_additions.headline: 5x Pro Bowl safety acquired for a 7th-round pick; immediately the Jets' best defensive player
Smith returns to his original team on a near-minimum bridge deal, solving 2026 QB at essentially no cost
Cost-controlled 24-year-old DT (PFF's 5th-ranked DT in 2025) acquired for Jermaine Johnson in a clear rebuild win
25-year-old ascending edge rusher signed as Jets' starter in new 3-4 front with career-high pressure metrics in 2025
Legendary LB returns for 3rd Jets stint under HC Aaron Glenn; elite run defender and locker-room leader at 37
Veteran run-stuffing DT pairs with Sweat to address Jets' 29th-ranked run defense
26-year-old ascending LG steps in to replace departed John Simpson at left guard
Ironman edge rusher (0 missed games in 4 years) adds depth to a thin EDGE room ahead of expected No. 2 pick
Best-value signing of the offseason: CB who tied for NFL-high 5 INTs in 2025 signed for near-minimum money
Elite tackler and special teams ace competes for S2 spot alongside Fitzpatrick

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded to Colts (2025 deadline) for 2 first-round picks
key_losses.headline: 2x All-Pro cornerstone traded in full teardown; CB1 remains the biggest unsolved hole entering the draft

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded to Cowboys (2025 deadline) for 2026 2nd, 2027 1st + DT Mazi Smith
key_losses.headline: All-Pro interior pass rusher traded in rebuild; T'Vondre Sweat and Onyemata are partial replacements

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded to Titans for T'Vondre Sweat (player-for-player)
key_losses.headline: Former first-rounder traded to clear path for top EDGE at No. 2 overall in 2026 NFL Draft
2-year starting LG departs on an overpay; Dylan Parham signed as direct replacement
Former first-round pick joins rival Patriots; three major injuries in 5 seasons made re-signing impractical
Former All-Pro LB departs after regression; Demario Davis signed as direct replacement and upgrade
NFL's most accurate kicker (96.3% since 2023) departs; Cade York signed as unproven replacement
